behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both test files, both test functions, the model IDs (Qwen2-7B, Qwen2-0.5B), the Expectations class with ('cuda', <memory_in_gb>) key format, and the decode/batch_decode method names.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what is broken (wrong tokenizer ID, plain string instead of Expectations, batch_decode for single items) without giving exact line-level patches.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_fixes_are_substantive asserts literal '("cuda", 8)' in qwen_src — an alternative correct fix using e.g. ('cuda', 7) or different memory value that's still valid would fail, even though instruction says '<memory_in_gb>' is flexible.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests parse AST, run ruff, py_compile, and repo check scripts — not just string matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require both removing Qwen2-7B tokenizer ref and adding Expectations with cuda key, and replacing batch_decode with decode — a stub cannot pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover tokenizer ID change, Expectations wrapper with cuda key, and decode vs batch_decode as described in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass) and descriptive names.

no_typos.reason: Paths, function names, and commands appear correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the test functions but not exact line numbers; localization within the function is reasonable given task scope.
